name: USERINPUT
description: User Input Script for Cursor AI with tkinter GUI. AI agents should execute USERINPUT separately to avoid output conflicts.
purpose: Get user feedback in Cursor AI workflows using a modern tkinter interface. AI agent should execute this command separately, not combined with other commands, to ensure feedback is not missed.
usage: USERINPUT: Pop up input window (claims from queue if available).
USERINPUT --enquiry: Bypass queue, request real-time user feedback.
USERINPUT --timeout <sec>: Set custom timeout (default 300s).
USERINPUT --id <id>: Use a custom session ID for identifying the instance.
USERINPUT --hint <text>: Pre-fill the text area with a hint for the user.
USERINPUT --queue: Add a prompt to the queue via GUI.
USERINPUT --queue --add <text>: Add a prompt to the queue directly.
USERINPUT --queue --delete <id>: Delete a queued prompt by index.
USERINPUT --queue --list: List all queued prompts.
USERINPUT --queue --gui: Open queue manager GUI.
USERINPUT --queue --move-up <id>: Move queued prompt up.
USERINPUT --queue --move-down <id>: Move queued prompt down.
USERINPUT --queue --move-to-top <id>: Move queued prompt to top.
USERINPUT --queue --move-to-bottom <id>: Move queued prompt to bottom.
USERINPUT --gui-submit --id <id>: Remotely submit an active instance.
USERINPUT --gui-cancel --id <id>: Remotely cancel an active instance.
USERINPUT --gui-add-time --id <id>: Remotely add 60s to an active instance.
USERINPUT --gui-stop --id <id>: Remotely terminate an active instance.
USERINPUT config --focus-interval <sec>: Set refocus/bell interval (default 90s).
USERINPUT config --time-increment <sec>: Set 'Add Time' button increment (default 60s).
USERINPUT config --list: List all system prompts.
USERINPUT config --gui: Open system prompt manager GUI.
USERINPUT config --add <str>: Add a system prompt.
USERINPUT config --delete <id>: Remove a system prompt by index.
USERINPUT config --move-up <id>: Move system prompt up.
USERINPUT config --move-down <id>: Move system prompt down.
USERINPUT config --move-to-top <id>: Move system prompt to top.
USERINPUT config --move-to-bottom <id>: Move system prompt to bottom.
